sections.summary: States that the standard applies to olive oils and olive‑pomace oils (as defined in Section 2) presented for human consumption.

Defines olive oil as oil obtained solely from the fruit of Olea europaea L. excluding solvent‑extracted or re‑esterified oils and blends, virgin olive oil as oil obtained only by mechanical/physical means without treatments other than washing, decanting, centrifuging and filtration, and olive‑pomace oil as oil recovered from olive pomace using non‑halogenated solvents or physical treatments, excluding re‑esterification and blends.

Establishes essential composition and quality framework for olive oil categories by setting designations/definitions and the compositional and analytical quality criteria (free acidity, physicochemical and organoleptic characteristics) applicable to the categories listed in Section 3.1.

Defines category designations (extra virgin, virgin, ordinary virgin, refined, refined olive‑pomace and blends), provides specific free acidity limits for each (e.g., extra virgin ≤0.8 g/100 g; virgin ≤2.0; ordinary virgin ≤3.3; refined and refined‑pomace ≤0.3; blends of refined and virgin ≤1.0), describes refined/refined‑pomace production, states that pomace blends shall not be called 'olive oil', and notes that Lampante is genuine virgin oil failing quality criteria and unfit for human consumption with a footnote on sale restrictions.

Lists the compositional factors and analytical parameters used to characterize olive oils (fatty acid profiles, trans isomers, ΔECN42, sterol composition and totals, erythrodiol/uvaol, waxes, stigmastadienes, 2‑MAG 2P, ΔK, etc.) and indicates their applicability to the categories in Section 3.1 and, in some cases, to Lampante and crude olive‑pomace oils for trade clarity.

sections.section: 3.2.1 GLC ranges of fatty acid composition (expressed as percentages of total fatty acids)
sections.summary: Provides GLC ranges for major fatty acids by oil category (e.g., C16:0 7.0–20.0%, C18:1 53.0–85.0%, C18:2 2.5–21.0%, C18:3 ≤1.0%) and sets limits on trans isomers (e.g., Σt‑C18:1 ≤0.05% in extra virgin with higher maxima for refined and pomace oils); values apply to oils for human consumption and, excluding trans isomers, may be used for Lampante/crude pomace trade.

sections.section: 3.2.2 Δ ECN42 (Difference between the actual and theoretical ECN 42 triglyceride content)
sections.summary: Specifies maximum absolute ΔECN42 (difference between actual and theoretical ECN42 triglyceride content) by category: extra virgin/virgin ≤|0.20|, refined and refined+virgin blends ≤|0.30|, and refined‑pomace and its blends ≤|0.50|.

Sets limits for 4α‑desmethylsterol composition: cholesterol ≤0.5%, brassicasterol ≤0.1% in olive oils (≤0.2% in olive‑pomace oils), campesterol ≤4.0%, stigmasterol < campesterol, Δ7‑stigmastenol ≤0.5%, and apparent β‑sitosterol ≥93.0% of total 4α‑desmethylsterols.

Requires minimum total 4α‑desmethylsterols content: ≥1,000 mg/kg for virgin and refined olive oil, ≥1,800 mg/kg for refined olive‑pomace oil, and ≥1,600 mg/kg for olive‑pomace oil composed of refined olive‑pomace oil and virgin olive oils.

sections.section: 3.2.5 Erythrodiol and uvaol  (% total 4α-desmethylsterols + erythrodiol and uvaol)
sections.summary: Limits erythrodiol and uvaol as a percentage of (total 4α‑desmethylsterols + erythrodiol + uvaol): ≤4.5% for extra virgin, virgin, refined and their blends, with values >4.5% characteristic of olive‑pomace/refined‑pomace oils.

Specifies wax content limits (mg/kg) by category: ≤150 for extra virgin/virgin, ≤350 for refined and refined/virgin blends, and >350 for refined‑pomace and pomace blends, with footnotes defining which ester sums (C40–C46) are measured.
Sets the stigmastadienes limit for extra virgin olive oil at ≤0.05 mg/kg.

Specifies maximum 2‑glyceryl monopalmitate (2P) percentages by category with conditional limits based on C16:0: for extra virgin/virgin/blends 2P ≤0.9% if C16:0 ≤14% (≤1.0% if >14%), refined oils slightly higher (up to 1.1%), refined‑pomace ≤1.4%, and pomace blends ≤1.2%.

Provides ΔK (spectrophotometric) requirements, giving virgin olive oil a limit of ≤0.01, defines the ΔK calculation (formula) and specifies measurement wavelengths (270 nm with cyclohexane or 268 nm with iso‑octane); some entries are retained until CCFO30.

Specifies quality factors across categories including organoleptic (median defect and fruity scores), chemical limits (free fatty acids, peroxide value), spectrophotometric limits (K270/K268, ΔK) and other analytical parameters, with different numeric limits for each oil category and notes where entries are retained.

sections.section: 3.3.1 Organoleptic characteristics of virgin olive oils
sections.summary: Defines sensory criteria for virgin oils: extra virgin must have median defect = 0 and median fruity > 0; virgin must have median defect ≤2.5 and median fruity > 0; ordinary virgin is defined by median defect >2.5 up to ≤6.0.

sections.section: 3.3.2 Free fatty acids (g/100 g, expressed as oleic acid)
sections.summary: Sets maximum free fatty acids (as g/100 g oleic acid) by category: extra virgin ≤0.8, virgin ≤2.0, refined ≤0.3, olive oil (refined+virgin) ≤1.0, and refined‑pomace ≤0.3.

sections.section: 3.3.3 Peroxide value (milliequivalents of active oxygen/kg oil)
sections.summary: Specifies peroxide value maxima (meq active O2/kg): extra virgin and virgin ≤20, refined and refined‑pomace ≤5, and blends (refined+virgin) and olive‑pomace blends ≤15.

sections.section: 3.3.4 Absorbency in the ultraviolet region (K270) at 270/or 268 nm (l)  (expressed as K270/or K268)
sections.summary: Provides K270/K268 UV absorbance limits by category (e.g., extra virgin ≤0.22; virgin ≤0.25; ordinary virgin ≤0.30; refined ≤1.25; refined+virgin ≤1.15; refined‑pomace ≤2.00; pomace blends ≤1.70).
Specifies ΔK limits for refined and pomace oils (refined ≤0.16; refined+virgin ≤0.15; refined‑pomace ≤0.20; pomace blends ≤0.18), and notes the ΔK definition and measurement wavelengths.
Heading for fatty acid ethyl esters referring to extra virgin olive oil; no numerical limit or substantive requirement is provided in this section.

Permits antioxidants listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 02.1.2 (vegetable oils and fats); however, no additives are permitted in virgin olive oils covered by this standard.

Products must comply with maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins (CXS 193-1995) and Codex MRLs for pesticide residues; maximum halogenated solvent limits are 0.1 mg/kg for each solvent and 0.2 mg/kg for the sum of all halogenated solvents.
For products covered by this standard, the maximum permitted content of each halogenated solvent is 0.1 mg/kg and the maximum permitted sum of all halogenated solvents is 0.2 mg/kg.
Recommends that products be prepared and handled according to the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Recommends that products be prepared and handled according to the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Requires that products be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).
Name of the product must be consistent with descriptions in Section 3 and the designation 'olive oil' must not be used to refer to olive‑pomace oils.
Labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
States that methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard shall be used to check compliance.

Defines quality characteristics for olive oils and olive‑pomace oils including organoleptic descriptors, limits for moisture/volatile matter, insoluble impurities, UV absorbance (K232) and trace metals (Fe ≤3.0 mg/kg, Cu ≤0.1 mg/kg), with specific values varying by oil category.
Provides organoleptic descriptors (odour, taste, colour) for refined, blended and pomace oil categories and refers extra virgin and virgin olive oil sensory criteria to Section 3.3.1.
Sets moisture and volatile matter limits: extra virgin and virgin olive oils ≤ 0.2 g/100 g; refined, blended and pomace oil categories ≤ 0.1 g/100 g.

sections.section: 1.3 Insoluble impurities in light petroleum (g/100 g)
sections.summary: Limits insoluble impurities in light petroleum to ≤ 0.1 g/100 g for extra virgin/virgin olive oils and ≤ 0.05 g/100 g for refined, blended and pomace oil categories.

sections.section: 1.4 Absorbance in the ultraviolet region at 232 nm ( expressed as K232)
sections.summary: Specifies UV absorbance limits at 232 nm (K232): extra virgin olive oil ≤ 2.50 and virgin olive oils ≤ 2.60.
Sets trace metal limits applicable to all olive oils and olive‑pomace oils: iron (Fe) ≤ 3.0 mg/kg and copper (Cu) ≤ 0.1 mg/kg.

Specifies chemical and physical parameter ranges by oil category including relative density (0.910–0.916), refractive index (≈1.4677–1.4707 depending on category), saponification value (182–196 mg KOH/g), iodine value (≈75–94) and unsaponifiable matter limits (≤15 g/kg for some categories, ≤30 g/kg for others).

sections.section: 2.1 Relative density (𝒅 𝒓 𝟐𝟎 ) (20 ºC/water at 20 ºC)
sections.summary: Defines relative density (d20r at 20 °C/water 20 °C) for the listed oil categories as 0.910–0.916.
Specifies refractive index ranges by oil category: extra virgin/virgin/refined/olive oil 1.4677–1.4705 and refined/olive‑pomace/pomace‑blend categories 1.4680–1.4707.
Specifies saponification value ranges (mg KOH/g) for olive oil categories: extra virgin/virgin/refined/olive‑oil blends 184–196 and refined olive‑pomace and its blends 182–193.
Sets iodine value (Wijs method) ranges for olive oil categories: extra virgin/virgin/refined/olive‑oil blends 75–94 and refined olive‑pomace and its blends 75–92.

Limits unsaponifiable matter (g/kg) in olive oils: extra virgin/virgin/refined olive oil ≤15 g/kg; olive oil composed of refined olive oil and virgin oils, refined olive‑pomace and olive‑pomace oil blends ≤30 g/kg.
Requires that methods of analysis and sampling listed in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234‑1999) relevant to this standard be used to check compliance.
